title: Incorrect Left/Right Boundary Handling in Binary Search

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Given a sorted array `nums` of distinct integers and a target integer `target`, find the index of the target in `nums` if it exists. The input will be handled in the following format in each language: [input count, arr elements, target]
statement.outputFormat: Index of the target in `nums` if it exists, otherwise -1
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(log n), where n is the size of `nums`. Space complexity: O(1)

hints.text: Carefully handle the case when the target is equal to the leftmost or rightmost element in the array.

starterCode.python:
class Solution:
    def binary_search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int:

functionSignature.java: public int binary_search(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int:
